message:
feat(search): Add query parser stress tests and SSH E2E test improvements

Query Parser Stress Tests (query.rs):
- Add stress_query_100k_chars_completes_quickly test ensuring <1s performance
- Add stress_query_1000_terms test with 1000 unique words
- Add stress_query_1000_identical_terms test with repeated same word
- Add stress_query_10k_char_single_term test for very long single tokens
- Add stress_deeply_nested_parentheses test for 100+ nesting levels
- Add stress_many_boolean_operators test for 100+ AND operators
- Add stress_alternating_operators test for mixed boolean operators
- Add stress_many_quoted_phrases test for 100+ phrase queries
- Add stress_unicode_mixed_scripts test for CJK, Arabic, emoji mix
- Add stress_special_char_boundaries test for control characters
- Add stress_whitespace_variations test for varied whitespace patterns
- Refactor existing test to use matches!() macro for cleaner assertions

SSH E2E Test Improvements (e2e_ssh_sources.rs):
- Add additional assertions and edge case handling
- Improve test documentation

TESTING.md Documentation:
- Document CASS_TRACE_FILE + CASS_TRACE_ID correlation in Rust E2E tests

Infrastructure:
- Update no_mock_audit.md with latest audit results
- Update e2e_jsonl_schema_test.rs with improvements

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1bqi","title":"[Feature] Performance Metrics Collection","description":"## Feature: Performance Metrics Collection

Emit performance metrics during E2E tests for regression detection.

### Metrics to Collect
| Metric | Unit | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `indexing_duration_ms` | ms | Time to index test corpus |
| `search_latency_p50_ms` | ms | Median search query time |
| `search_latency_p99_ms` | ms | 99th percentile search time |
| `memory_peak_kb` | KB | Peak memory usage |
| `index_size_bytes` | bytes | Size of search index |
| `files_processed` | count | Number of files indexed |
| `queries_per_second` | qps | Search throughput |

### Implementation
```rust
// In tests/util/e2e_log.rs
pub fn emit_metric(name: &str, value: f64, unit: &str) -> Result<()> {
    let event = json\!({
        "ts": Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
        "event": "metric",
        "name": name,
        "value": value,
        "unit": unit,
        "run_id": run_id()
    });
    writeln\!(log_file(), "{}", event)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### Usage
```rust
let start = Instant::now();
indexer.index_all()?;
emit_metric("indexing_duration_ms", start.elapsed().as_millis() as f64, "ms")?;
```

### Baseline Tracking
- Store baselines in `test-results/baselines.json`
- Alert if metric exceeds baseline by >20%
- Update baseline after confirmed improvements

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `emit_metric` function in e2e_log
- [ ] 7 metrics collected per E2E run
- [ ] Baseline comparison implemented
- [ ] Alert on regression >20%
- [ ] Metrics visible in JSONL output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:23:59.502864770Z","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:10:40.223891248Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1bqi","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ohe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:26:10.915148705Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1d42ae1e","title":"Cursor-Based Pagination","description":"# Cursor-Based Pagination

## Problem Statement
Offset-based pagination (`--offset N`) has issues:
- Results can shift if index changes between pages
- Hard to track position reliably
- Inefficient for large offsets

## Proposed Solution
Add cursor-based pagination:
```bash
cass search "error" --json --limit 10
# Returns: {"next_cursor": "abc123", "hits": [...]}

cass search "error" --json --limit 10 --cursor "abc123"
# Returns next page
```

## Design Decisions

### Cursor Encoding
Cursor contains:
- Query hash (to validate same query)
- Last seen sort key (score + id)
- Expiration timestamp

Encoded as base64 for URL safety.

### Cursor Expiration
Cursors expire after 1 hour to prevent stale state issues.

### Fallback
If cursor is invalid/expired, return error with hint to restart.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--cursor` parameter for continuing pagination
- [ ] `next_cursor` in response when more results available
- [ ] Cursor validates query hasn't changed
- [ ] Expired cursors return clear error
- [ ] Works with all output formats

## Effort Estimate
Medium - 3-4 hours. Requires cursor encoding and validation logic.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.002460810Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T05:04:40.516945Z","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1dlw","title":"T2: Mock Elimination - Replace Transitional Mocks","description":"# Epic: Eliminate Transitional Mocks

## Goal
Remove all transitional mock patterns from the codebase, replacing them with real implementations or test fixtures.

## Current Transitional Mocks (from no_mock_allowlist.json)
1. tests/connector_claude.rs - mock_claude variable
2. tests/fs_errors.rs - mock_claude variable
3. tests/pages_bundle.rs - fake_config

## Review Deadline
All transitional mocks scheduled for removal by 2026-03-26

## Approach
- Create real fixture directories from actual session data
- Use ConversationFixtureBuilder for deterministic test data
- Replace fake configs with real PagesConfig instances

## Dependencies
- Blocked by T1 (need unit tests before removing mocks)
- Referenced in downstream task bd-1dfc","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:19:57.403736141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:27:49.637335096Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:27:49.637268994Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1dlw","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3fbl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:22:10.386929338Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2v0a","title":"[Task] Create security_paths_e2e.sh E2E Script","description":"## Task: Create Security Paths E2E Script

Create `scripts/e2e/security_paths_e2e.sh` that validates path security in the export pipeline.

### SECURITY CRITICAL - P0

### Purpose
Validate that path traversal protections work in the full export/verify pipeline:
- Export sessions with attempted path traversal
- Verify integrity checks catch attacks
- Confirm no files written outside target directory

### Test Scenarios
1. **Basic traversal** - `../../../etc/passwd` in integrity.json
2. **URL encoding** - `%2e%2e%2f` variants
3. **Unicode variants** - Fullwidth dots, homoglyphs
4. **Null byte injection** - `valid%00/../attack`
5. **Symlink attacks** - Symlinks pointing outside
6. **Race conditions** - TOCTOU attacks

### Script Structure
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source scripts/lib/e2e_log.sh

e2e_init "shell" "security_paths_e2e"
e2e_run_start

# Setup: Create test environment
e2e_phase_start "setup" "Creating isolated test environment"
TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
ATTACK_TARGET="/etc/passwd"
trap "rm -rf $TEST_DIR" EXIT
e2e_phase_end "setup"

# Test: Basic traversal blocked
e2e_phase_start "basic_traversal" "Testing basic path traversal"
e2e_test_start "basic_traversal_blocked" "security"
create_malicious_integrity "$TEST_DIR" "../../../etc/passwd"
if \! cass pages verify "$TEST_DIR" 2>&1 | grep -q "security violation"; then
    e2e_test_fail "basic_traversal_blocked" "security" "Traversal not detected" 0
    exit 1  # Security test failure is fatal
else
    e2e_test_pass "basic_traversal_blocked" "security" "$duration"
fi
e2e_phase_end "basic_traversal"

# Test: URL encoding blocked
e2e_phase_start "url_encoding" "Testing URL-encoded traversal"
# ... tests ...
e2e_phase_end "url_encoding"

# Verify no unauthorized file access
e2e_phase_start "audit" "Auditing file access"
# Check that no files outside TEST_DIR were accessed
e2e_phase_end "audit"

e2e_run_end "$total" "$passed" "$failed" "$skipped" "$total_duration"
```

### Security Requirements
- Script runs in isolated tmpdir
- Failure on ANY security test = script exits non-zero
- All attacks logged with full details
- No actual files modified outside test dir

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script at `scripts/e2e/security_paths_e2e.sh`
- [ ] 6 attack categories tested
- [ ] ALL attacks detected and blocked
- [ ] Script exits non-zero on security failure
- [ ] Detailed logging of attack attempts

### Verification
```bash
./scripts/e2e/security_paths_e2e.sh
echo "Exit code: $?"  # Must be 0
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:08:58.368946216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:59:33.458695885Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:59:33.458551006Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2v0a","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-6xnm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:09:26.226551827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2w4","title":"P1.1 Define Source/Origin Rust types","description":"# Define Source/Origin Rust Types

## Context
We need Rust types to represent "where a conversation came from" - the provenance system. These types will be used throughout cass: storage, indexing, search, CLI, TUI.

## Location
Create new module: src/model/source.rs (or src/sources.rs if we prefer top-level)

## Types to Define

### SourceKind enum
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum SourceKind {
    Local,
    Ssh,
    // Future: S3, Git, Http
}
\`\`\`

### Source struct (configuration-level)
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Source {
    /// Stable, user-friendly identifier (e.g., "local", "work-laptop", "home-server")
    pub id: String,
    
    /// What type of source this is
    pub kind: SourceKind,
    
    /// Display label for UI (often SSH alias or hostname)
    pub host_label: Option<String>,
    
    /// Optional stable machine identifier (hashed if desired)
    pub machine_id: Option<String>,
    
    /// Platform hint for path defaults
    pub platform: Option<Platform>,
    
    /// Arbitrary config (SSH params, path rewrites, etc)
    pub config: serde_json::Value,
    
    pub created_at: Option<i64>,
    pub updated_at: Option<i64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum Platform {
    Macos,
    Linux,
    Windows,
}
\`\`\`

### Origin struct (per-conversation metadata)
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Origin {
    /// References Source.id
    pub source_id: String,
    
    /// Denormalized for convenience
    pub kind: SourceKind,
    
    /// Display host label (may differ from source's host_label)
    pub host: Option<String>,
}

impl Default for Origin {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            source_id: "local".into(),
            kind: SourceKind::Local,
            host: None,
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Constants
\`\`\`rust
pub const LOCAL_SOURCE_ID: &str = "local";
\`\`\`

## Considerations
- Use serde for JSON serialization (robot output compatibility)
- Derive common traits: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash where appropriate
- Document public types with doc comments
- Consider implementing Display for user-friendly formatting

## Wire into lib.rs
Export these types from the crate root so they're accessible:
\`\`\`rust
pub mod sources;
pub use sources::{Source, SourceKind, Origin, Platform, LOCAL_SOURCE_ID};
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Types defined with proper derives
- [ ] Doc comments on all public items
- [ ] Unit tests for Default impls and serialization
- [ ] Exported from crate root
- [ ] cargo check passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:54:10.114500Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T06:51:13.559723Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T06:51:13.559723Z","close_reason":"Implemented SourceKind, Source, Origin, SourceFilter types with LOCAL_SOURCE_ID constant in src/sources/provenance.rs. Refactored config.rs to use shared SourceKind instead of duplicate SourceConnectionType. 34 unit tests pass. cargo check/clippy/fmt all pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2w4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-495","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:56:03.359071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2w98","title":"[Task] Remaining Connectors Edge Case Tests","description":"## Task: Remaining Connectors Edge Case Tests

Apply the edge case test pattern established in Claude connector to ALL remaining connectors.

### Connectors to Cover
- [ ] `src/connectors/cursor.rs` - Cursor IDE sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/aider.rs` - Aider CLI sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/cline.rs` - Cline plugin sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/amp.rs` - Amp sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/opencode.rs` - OpenCode sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/pi_agent.rs` - Pi-Agent sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/goose.rs` - Goose sessions

### Per-Connector Test Requirements
Each connector must have:
1. **Standard edge cases** (10 tests from Claude pattern):
   - Truncated JSON, Invalid UTF-8, Empty file, etc.
2. **Connector-specific edge cases** (3-5 tests):
   - Format quirks unique to that agent

### Implementation Approach
1. Create `#[cfg(test)] mod edge_case_tests` in each file
2. Copy standard test helpers from Claude
3. Add connector-specific cases

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-395v","title":"[Feature] Verbose Debug Logging Mode","description":"## Feature: Verbose Debug Logging Mode

Implement `E2E_VERBOSE=1` environment variable that enables detailed step-by-step logging.

### What It Does
When enabled, tests emit detailed debug information:
- Every file operation logged
- Every command with full arguments
- Every assertion with context
- Every state transition

### Implementation
1. **Environment check** - Check `E2E_VERBOSE` at test start
2. **Conditional logging** - `if verbose { log_debug\!(...) }`
3. **Separate output file** - `test-results/e2e/verbose_*.log`
4. **No JSONL impact** - Verbose logs are text, not JSONL events

### Usage
```bash
E2E_VERBOSE=1 ./scripts/e2e/connector_stress.sh
# Creates: test-results/e2e/verbose_connector_stress.log
```

### Rust Implementation
```rust
fn verbose_log(msg: &str) {
    if std::env::var("E2E_VERBOSE").is_ok() {
        eprintln\!("[VERBOSE] {}", msg);
        // Also append to verbose log file
    }
}
```

### Shell Implementation
```bash
verbose_log() {
    if [ -n "${E2E_VERBOSE:-}" ]; then
        echo "[VERBOSE] $*" | tee -a "$VERBOSE_LOG"
    fi
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `E2E_VERBOSE=1` enables verbose mode
- [ ] Verbose logs go to separate file
- [ ] Does not affect JSONL event stream
- [ ] All E2E scripts support verbose mode
- [ ] Verbose output includes timestamps","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:23:59.502864770Z","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:10:04.233534331Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-395v","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ohe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:26:08.170154256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3a51","title":"P4.1c: Integrity Fingerprint System","description":"# P4.1b: Integrity Fingerprint System

## Goal
Implement the integrity fingerprint system that enables out-of-band verification of bundle integrity, protecting against repo compromise or asset tampering attacks.

## Why This Task is Critical

Section 7.1 of the plan identifies bundle tampering as a key threat:
- If an attacker modifies viewer.js/index.html, they can steal passwords
- GitHub Pages cannot prevent repo compromises
- Users need a way to verify integrity before entering passwords

This provides defense-in-depth for high-trust sharing scenarios.

## Technical Implementation

### Generated Files

```
site/
├── integrity.json           # SHA-256 hashes of all public files
└── ...

private/
├── integrity-fingerprint.txt  # SHA-256(integrity.json) - share out-of-band
└── ...
```

### integrity.json Structure

```json
{
    "version": 1,
    "generated_at": "2025-01-06T12:34:56Z",
    "cass_version": "0.1.55",
    "files": {
        "index.html": "sha256-abc123def456...",
        "app.js": "sha256-789ghi012jkl...",
        "app.css": "sha256-mno345pqr678...",
        "sw.js": "sha256-stu901vwx234...",
        "config.json": "sha256-yza567bcd890...",
        "wasm/sql-wasm.wasm": "sha256-efg123hij456...",
        "payload/chunk-00000.bin": "sha256-klm789nop012...",
        "payload/chunk-00001.bin": "sha256-qrs345tuv678..."
    },
    "fingerprint": "sha256-OVERALL_HASH..."
}
```

### Rust Implementation

```rust
// src/pages/integrity.rs
use sha2::{Sha256, Digest};
use std::collections::BTreeMap;

pub struct IntegrityGenerator {
    files: BTreeMap<String, String>,  // path -> hash
}

impl IntegrityGenerator {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self { files: BTreeMap::new() }
    }
    
    pub fn add_file(&mut self, path: &str, content: &[u8]) {
        let hash = format!("sha256-{}", hex::encode(Sha256::digest(content)));
        self.files.insert(path.to_string(), hash);
    }
    
    pub fn generate(&self) -> (IntegrityManifest, String) {
        let manifest = IntegrityManifest {
            version: 1,
            generated_at: Utc::now(),
            cass_version: env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").to_string(),
            files: self.files.clone(),
            fingerprint: String::new(),  // Computed below
        };
        
        // Compute manifest JSON
        let manifest_json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&manifest).unwrap();
        
        // Compute fingerprint of manifest
        let fingerprint = format!("sha256-{}", hex::encode(Sha256::digest(&manifest_json)));
        
        // Update manifest with fingerprint
        let final_manifest = IntegrityManifest {
            fingerprint: fingerprint.clone(),
            ..manifest
        };
        
        (final_manifest, fingerprint)
    }
    
    pub fn write_files(&self, site_dir: &Path, private_dir: &Path) -> Result<(), Error> {
        let (manifest, fingerprint) = self.generate();
        
        // Write integrity.json to site/
        let manifest_json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&manifest)?;
        fs::write(site_dir.join("integrity.json"), &manifest_json)?;
        
        // Write fingerprint to private/
        let fingerprint_text = format!(
            "CASS Archive Integrity Fingerprint\n\
             ==================================\n\n\
             Share this fingerprint out-of-band with trusted recipients.\n\
             They should verify it matches before entering the password.\n\n\
             Fingerprint: {}\n\n\
             Generated: {}\n\
             CASS Version: {}\n",
            fingerprint,
            Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
            env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
        );
        fs::write(private_dir.join("integrity-fingerprint.txt"), fingerprint_text)?;
        
        info!("Integrity fingerprint: {}", fingerprint);
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Browser-Side TOFU Verification

```javascript
// web/src/integrity.js

class IntegrityVerifier {
    constructor() {
        this.storedFingerprint = localStorage.getItem("cass_fingerprint");
    }
    
    async fetchAndVerify() {
        try {
            const response = await fetch("./integrity.json");
            const manifest = await response.json();
            
            // Compute hash of manifest (minus the fingerprint field)
            const toHash = { ...manifest };
            delete toHash.fingerprint;
            const computed = await sha256(JSON.stringify(toHash));
            
            if (computed !== manifest.fingerprint) {
                return { valid: false, reason: "Manifest self-check failed" };
            }
            
            // Check for TOFU violation
            if (this.storedFingerprint && this.storedFingerprint !== manifest.fingerprint) {
                return { 
                    valid: false, 
                    reason: "TOFU_VIOLATION",
                    previousFingerprint: this.storedFingerprint,
                    currentFingerprint: manifest.fingerprint
                };
            }
            
            return { 
                valid: true, 
                fingerprint: manifest.fingerprint,
                isFirstVisit: !this.storedFingerprint
            };
            
        } catch (error) {
            return { valid: false, reason: error.message };
        }
    }
    
    storeFingerprint(fingerprint) {
        localStorage.setItem("cass_fingerprint", fingerprint);
    }
    
    clearStoredFingerprint() {
        localStorage.removeItem("cass_fingerprint");
    }
}

// Display fingerprint before password entry
async function showIntegrityCheck() {
    const verifier = new IntegrityVerifier();
    const result = await verifier.fetchAndVerify();
    
    const container = document.getElementById("integrity-status");
    
    if (!result.valid) {
        if (result.reason === "TOFU_VIOLATION") {
            container.innerHTML = \`
                <div class="integrity-warning">
                    <h3>⚠️ Bundle Changed Since Last Visit</h3>
                    <p>The archive files have been modified since you last visited.</p>
                    <p>Previous: <code>\${result.previousFingerprint.slice(0, 16)}...</code></p>
                    <p>Current: <code>\${result.currentFingerprint.slice(0, 16)}...</code></p>
                    <p>If you did not expect this change, DO NOT enter your password.</p>
                    <button onclick="acceptNewFingerprint()">I trust this change</button>
                </div>
            \`;
            return false;
        }
        
        container.innerHTML = \`
            <div class="integrity-error">
                <p>⚠️ Integrity check failed: \${result.reason}</p>
            </div>
        \`;
        return false;
    }
    
    // Show fingerprint for verification
    const shortFp = result.fingerprint.slice(7, 23);  // First 16 hex chars after "sha256-"
    container.innerHTML = \`
        <div class="integrity-ok">
            <p>🔐 Fingerprint: <code title="\${result.fingerprint}">\${shortFp}...</code></p>
            <p class="hint">Verify this matches what the archive owner sent you.</p>
        </div>
    \`;
    
    // Store on first visit
    if (result.isFirstVisit) {
        verifier.storeFingerprint(result.fingerprint);
    }
    
    return true;
}
```

### UI Integration

```html
<!-- In auth UI, before password input -->
<div id="integrity-status" class="integrity-container">
    <p>Checking integrity...</p>
</div>

<form id="auth-form">
    <input type="password" id="password" placeholder="Enter password">
    <button type="submit">Unlock</button>
</form>
```

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_integrity_generation() {
        let mut gen = IntegrityGenerator::new();
        gen.add_file("index.html", b"<html>...</html>");
        gen.add_file("app.js", b"function main() {}");
        
        let (manifest, fingerprint) = gen.generate();
        
        assert_eq!(manifest.version, 1);
        assert_eq!(manifest.files.len(), 2);
        assert!(fingerprint.starts_with("sha256-"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_fingerprint_deterministic() {
        let mut gen1 = IntegrityGenerator::new();
        gen1.add_file("a.txt", b"hello");
        gen1.add_file("b.txt", b"world");
        
        let mut gen2 = IntegrityGenerator::new();
        gen2.add_file("b.txt", b"world");
        gen2.add_file("a.txt", b"hello");
        
        let (_, fp1) = gen1.generate();
        let (_, fp2) = gen2.generate();
        
        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);  // Order doesnt matter (BTreeMap)
    }
}
```

### E2E Tests

```javascript
describe("Integrity Verification", () => {
    test("first visit stores fingerprint", async () => {
        localStorage.clear();
        
        await showIntegrityCheck();
        
        expect(localStorage.getItem("cass_fingerprint")).toBeTruthy();
    });

    test("detects TOFU violation", async () => {
        localStorage.setItem("cass_fingerprint", "sha256-old");
        
        const result = await new IntegrityVerifier().fetchAndVerify();
        
        expect(result.valid).toBe(false);
        expect(result.reason).toBe("TOFU_VIOLATION");
    });
});
```

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3ci5","title":"[Task] Audit Plain assert! Calls","description":"## Task: Audit Plain assert\! Calls

Find and improve all plain `assert\!()` and `assert_eq\!()` calls without context messages.

### Scope
- `tests/*.rs` - Integration tests
- `src/**/tests/` - Unit test modules

### What to Find
```bash
# Plain assert without message
rg 'assert\!\([^,]+\);' tests/ --type rust

# Plain assert_eq without message  
rg 'assert_eq\!\([^,]+,[^,]+\);' tests/ --type rust

# Plain assert_ne without message
rg 'assert_ne\!\([^,]+,[^,]+\);' tests/ --type rust
```

### Transform Pattern
```rust
// BEFORE: No context
assert\!(result.is_ok());
assert_eq\!(count, 5);

// AFTER: With context
assert\!(result.is_ok(), 
    "parse_message failed for input {:?}: {:?}", 
    input, result.err());
assert_eq\!(count, 5, 
    "expected 5 messages but got {} for fixture {}", 
    count, fixture_name);
```

### Guidelines
1. **Include the "what"** - What was being tested
2. **Include the "expected"** - What should have happened
3. **Include the "actual"** - What actually happened (use `{:?}`)
4. **Include context** - Which fixture, input, or scenario

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All plain assert\! calls have context messages
- [ ] Messages explain expected vs actual
- [ ] Fixture/input included where relevant
- [ ] At least 50 assertions improved
- [ ] No broken tests after changes

### Verification
```bash
# Should return 0 results after completion
rg 'assert\!\([^,]+\);' tests/ --type rust | wc -l
cargo test

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e28","title":"cass models CLI command","description":"## Purpose
CLI subcommand for model management (pre-provisioning, verification, cleanup, offline install).

## Commands
```bash
# Show model status
cass models status [--json]

# Install/download model (consent via CLI)
cass models install [--model all-minilm-l6-v2] [--mirror URL] [--from-file PATH]

# Verify model integrity
cass models verify [--repair]

# Remove model files
cass models remove [--model all-minilm-l6-v2] [-y]

# Check for updates (does not auto-download)
cass models check-update [--json]

# Upgrade to latest pinned version
cass models upgrade [--model all-minilm-l6-v2]
```

## Offline Installation (--from-file)
For truly air-gapped environments where even mirrors aren't accessible:
```bash
# On connected machine: download model package
cass models download --output ~/model-package.tar.gz

# On air-gapped machine: install from file
cass models install --from-file ~/model-package.tar.gz
```

Package format:
- tar.gz containing model files + manifest.json with checksums
- SHA256 verified even for file installs

## Use Cases
- Pre-provision model before first TUI use
- Verify model in CI/automated environments
- Cleanup disk space
- Install with custom mirror for restricted networks
- Fully offline installation via file transfer

## JSON Output (for scripting)
```json
{
  "state": "ready",
  "model_id": "all-minilm-l6-v2",
  "model_path": "/Users/x/.local/share/coding-agent-search/models/all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
  "revision": "e4ce9877abf3edfe10b0d82785e83bdcb973e22e",
  "size_bytes": 23000000,
  "verified": true,
  "update_available": false
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands work correctly
- [ ] JSON output is parseable
- [ ] Install works in headless/CI environments
- [ ] Verify catches and reports corruption
- [ ] Remove prompts before deletion (unless -y)
- [ ] --from-file installs from local package
- [ ] --mirror overrides default HuggingFace URL

## Depends On
- sem.mod.core (Model management)
